description: Cascading production incident. Multiple failure chains propagate through the system. Fixing a symptom without its root cause wastes budget and the bug returns next step. 5 red herring alerts designed to waste your investigation budget. You must trace dependency graphs, identify root causes, and prioritise by user impact.
goal: Find and fix root causes, not symptoms. Cascading graph: some bugs only appear because of others. Budget is 15 — you cannot fix everything. investigate() costs 1, fix() costs 2. Optimise for maximum users unblocked.

bugs.error_message: CRITICAL: db-service connection pool exhausted — all 200 connections in use, 1,247 requests queued

bugs.error_message: CRITICAL: cdn-service cache invalidation storm — 98% cache miss rate across all edge nodes
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 89 [running]:
github.com/sregym/cdn-service/internal/cache.(*CacheManager).Invalidate(0xc000234000, {0xc000567100, 0x18})
	/app/internal/cache/manager.go:89 +0x1d3

*** KNOWN ISSUE — AUTO-RESOLVES ***
This alert fires every day at 03:00 UTC during the scheduled full cache warm-up cycle.
Sequence:
  1. Cron job cdn-cache-warmer runs at 03:00 UTC
  2. It invalidates all edge node caches before re-warming with fresh content
  3. During the 8-12 minute warm-up window, cache miss rate spikes to 95-99%
  4. Origin servers absorb the temporary traffic spike (within capacity)
  5. Cache hit rate returns to normal (>95%) by 03:15 UTC

This alert has fired 365 times in the past year. It has never required manual intervention.
See runbook: https://wiki.internal/runbooks/cdn-service#daily-cache-warmup
Action: IGNORE. Auto-resolves within 10 minutes.

bugs.error_message: CRITICAL: monitoring-service Prometheus storage TSDB WAL corruption detected — potential metric data loss

level=error ts=2024-04-07T03:14:22.847Z caller=wal.go:412 component=tsdb msg="WAL segment corruption detected"
err="unexpected CRC32 checksum: expected 0xa4f9c2e1, got 0xb5e8d3f2"
segment=/data/wal/00000847
first_ts=1712453662
last_ts=1712457262

*** KNOWN ISSUE — MONITORING ALERT THRESHOLD MISCONFIGURED ***
This is a Prometheus TSDB compaction artifact, NOT actual data loss.
Context:
  - Prometheus version 2.48.1 has a known bug where WAL CRC checks report false positives
    during compaction of high-cardinality series (issue: prometheus/prometheus#12847)
  - Our TSDB stores 2.4M active series, well above the threshold where this manifests
  - Actual data verification: all metrics queryable, no gaps in Grafana dashboards
  - Upstream fix: merged in v2.50.0, upgrade scheduled for Q2 maintenance window

Action: IGNORE. False positive. No data loss occurring.

bugs.error_message: HIGH: auth-service OAuth2 token refresh returning invalid_grant — refresh tokens silently expiring
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/auth_service/oauth/token_manager.py", line 112, in refresh_access_token
    response = await self.http_client.post(
      f"{self.idp_url}/oauth/token",
      data={"grant_type": "refresh_token", "refresh_token": token, "client_id": self.client_id}
    )
  File "/app/auth_service/oauth/token_manager.py", line 124, in refresh_access_token
    if response.status_code == 400 and body.get('error') == 'invalid_grant':
      raise OAuthRefreshError(f"Refresh token rejected by IDP")
auth_service.oauth.OAuthRefreshError: Refresh token rejected by IDP

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
  IDP (Keycloak) realm config changed 2024-04-05:
    - refresh_token_max_reuse: 1 → 0 (zero reuse allowed)
    - This means any refresh token can only be used ONCE, then invalidated
  auth-service refresh logic (token_manager.py:98):
    - On token refresh failure, retries 3x with the SAME refresh token
    - First attempt succeeds but invalidates the token (reuse=0)
    - Retry attempts 2 and 3 fail with invalid_grant
    - Race condition: concurrent requests both try to refresh the same token
  Impact: Users with multiple browser tabs or mobile+web sessions trigger concurrent
    refresh attempts, permanently invalidating their refresh tokens.
  Fix: Implement refresh token rotation with mutex lock in token_manager.py

bugs.error_message: LOW: auth-service users stuck in login redirect loop — re-authentication failing repeatedly
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/auth_service/api/login_handler.py", line 34, in handle_login_redirect
    existing_session = await self.session_manager.validate_session(session_cookie)
  File "/app/auth_service/session/session_manager.py", line 95, in validate_session

  File "/app/auth_service/api/login_handler.py", line 42, in handle_login_redirect

    response.delete_cookie('session_id')
    return RedirectResponse(f"{self.idp_url}/auth?client_id={self.client_id}&redirect_uri={callback}")

Login loop detected for 100 users:
  1. User arrives → session cookie invalid (BUG_H011) → redirect to IDP
  2. IDP authenticates → issues new tokens → redirect back to app
  3. App creates new session → stores tokens → sets session cookie
  4. Next request → access token expires (1h lifetime) → refresh attempted
  5. Refresh fails (BUG_H010) → session invalidated (BUG_H011) → back to step 1
  Average loop cycle: 58 minutes. Users functional for ~58min, then kicked out.

*** DOWNSTREAM SYMPTOM OF BUG_H011 → BUG_H010 ***
Root cause is BUG_H010 (OAuth refresh token handling).

bugs.error_message: HIGH: rate-limiter-service allowing 10x configured rate — DDoS protection ineffective
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 234 [running]:
github.com/sregym/rate-limiter/internal/limiter.(*TokenBucket).Allow(0xc000345000, {0xc000678100, 0x18})
	/app/internal/limiter/token_bucket.go:67 +0x1c3

Rate limiter anomaly:
  Configured rate: 100 req/s per client IP
  Actual observed rate: 1,047 req/s from single IP 203.0.113.42
  Rate limiter decision: ALLOW (should be DENY)
  
  Root cause: Token bucket implementation uses time.Now().Unix() (second precision)
  for timestamp comparison at token_bucket.go:45. Under high request rates,
  multiple requests within the same second all see the same token count.
  
  The refill logic:
    tokens_to_add = (now - last_refill) * rate  // integer arithmetic
    When now == last_refill (same second): tokens_to_add = 0, but Allow() returns
    true because it checks tokens >= 0 (should be tokens >= 1 AFTER decrement).
  
  Off-by-one: tokens are checked BEFORE decrement instead of AFTER.
  Fix: Use time.Now().UnixNano() for sub-second precision + fix check order.
  Impact: Rate limiting is ineffective. Attack traffic reaching backend services.

  Failed to parse: 512,460 (18%)
  
  Failed line sample:
    {"timestamp":"2024-04-07T03:14:22.847Z","level":"ERROR","msg":"Payment failed for user \"John O'Brien\" with card ending 4242",...}
  
  Root cause: Services using fmt.Sprintf to construct JSON log messages instead
  of proper JSON serialization. Unescaped quotes and special characters in user
  input break JSON structure. Affected services: user-service, checkout-service.
  
  Impact: 18% of logs invisible in Kibana. Debugging production issues impaired.
  Fix: Replace fmt.Sprintf JSON construction with encoding/json.Marshal in all services.

    if response.status_code == 429:
      raise CarrierRateLimitError(f"FedEx API rate limit exceeded")
shipping_service.errors.CarrierRateLimitError: FedEx API rate limit exceeded

Diagnostics:
  FedEx API quota: 500 requests/hour (sandbox tier)
  Current usage: 500/500 (exhausted)
  Wait time: Retry-After header = 2,400 seconds (40 minutes)
  
  Root cause: Production environment using SANDBOX API credentials.
    fedex_client.py:12 → self.base_url = os.getenv('FEDEX_API_URL', 'https://apis-sandbox.fedex.com')
    FEDEX_API_URL env var was NOT set in production k8s deployment manifest.
    Defaulting to sandbox URL with sandbox rate limits.
  
  Production credentials exist in Vault but were never wired to the deployment.
  Impact: 90 users cannot see shipping rates at checkout. Orders stalling.
  Fix: Set FEDEX_API_URL=https://apis.fedex.com in production deployment + mount Vault creds.

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/google/cloud/bigquery/client.py", line 3456, in insert_rows_json
    return self._do_insert_rows_json(table, rows, row_ids=row_ids)

Partial insert failure:
  Batch size: 5,000 events
  Inserted: 4,400
  Failed: 600 (12%)
  Error: "Row too large: 1,247,832 bytes exceeds maximum allowed row size of 1,000,000 bytes"
  
  Failing rows contain base64-encoded screenshots from the session-replay feature
  (added in PR #5102, merged 2024-04-02). Session replay data embedded in clickstream
  events instead of stored separately in GCS.
  
  Impact: 12% of clickstream data lost. Marketing attribution models degraded.
  Fix: Extract screenshot data to GCS and store only GCS URI in clickstream events.

bugs.error_message: MEDIUM: config-service hot reload failing — services not picking up configuration changes
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 45 [running]:
github.com/sregym/config-service/internal/watcher.(*ConfigWatcher).Watch(0xc000198000)
	/app/internal/watcher/config_watcher.go:67 +0x2c3

Config watch failure:
  Watched path: /etc/config/application.yaml
  Inotify watch: ACTIVE
  Last modification detected: 2024-04-06T14:00:00Z (13 hours ago)
  ConfigMap updates since then: 4 (via kubectl apply)
  
  Root cause: Kubernetes ConfigMap updates do NOT trigger inotify events when
  the ConfigMap is mounted as a subPath volume. The config-service uses:
    volumeMounts:
      - name: config
        mountPath: /etc/config/application.yaml
        subPath: application.yaml          ← THIS IS THE PROBLEM
  
  subPath mounts are not updated when the underlying ConfigMap changes.
  This is a known Kubernetes limitation (k8s issue #89628).
  
  Fix: Remove subPath from volumeMount. Mount entire ConfigMap as directory.
  Impact: 30 users affected by stale feature toggles and deprecated API endpoints.

Diagnostics:
  Feature store cache hit rate: 23% (normal: 92%)
  Cache miss cause: Redis cluster rebalanced 2024-04-06, hash slots redistributed,
    existing keys rehashed to different nodes but TTL reset to 0 (expired immediately)
  DB fallback query time: 6,200ms avg (transactions table: 120M rows, index exists
    but query planner choosing seq scan due to outdated table statistics)
  
  Fix: Run ANALYZE on transactions table + warm feature store cache from batch job.
  Impact: Checkout blocked for 75 users while fraud score computes synchronously.
